title: Simple Formylation of Aromatic Compounds Using a Sodium Formate/Triphenylphosphine Ditriflate System
author: Mohammad M. Khodaei
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-2.0
description: A new procedure was developed for formylation of arenes to produce aromatic aldehydes using a sodium formate/triphenylphosphine ditriflate system in ethanol at room temperature in good yields. The simplicity of the procedure, short reaction times, and mild reaction conditions are the other advantages of this metal- and carbon monoxide-free protocol.
steps_raw: A new procedure was developed for formylation of arenes to produce aromatic aldehydes using a sodium formate/triphenylphosphine ditriflate system in ethanol at room temperature in good yields. The simplicity of the procedure, short reaction times, and mild reaction conditions are the other advantages of this metal- and carbon monoxide-free protocol.
keywords: Formylation of arene, Metal- and CO-free protocol, Aromatic aldehyde
